target.name: Form Energy
target.founder: Mateo Jaramillo, Yet-Ming Chiang, Ted Wiley, William Woodford, Marco Ferrara

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $3.5B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $1.8B - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: TAE Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series G), post-money $6B

private.name: Watershed
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series G), post-money $1.8B

data.narrative:
Form Energy, a Series G climate and energy technology company, is valued at $3.5B post-money following $690M+ in total capital raised. The peer universe comprises nine comparable companies across the climate and energy sector, including four direct stage peers (TAE Technologies, Helion, Sila Nanotechnologies, and Watershed) alongside a broader set of late-stage private innovators and one public reference point.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using a 1.25x step-up multiplier typical for Series G advancement, implies a valuation of $4.4B. Peer-median methodology across the four same-sector and same/adjacent-stage comparables yields $4.3B, with the peer set ranging from $1.8B (Watershed) to $6.0B (TAE Technologies). Both methods cluster tightly, suggesting convergent signal around $4.3B–$4.4B.

Triangulating across these two methods produces a valuation range of $3.7B (low) to $5.0B (high), with a median implied value of $4.4B. This range sits modestly above Form Energy's current $3.5B valuation and reflects the typical step-up profile for late-stage climate-tech companies. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion within the peer set ($1.8B–$6.0B), reflecting heterogeneity in technology maturity and commercialization timelines within the sector, and the limited public market comparability for validation.
